claim: Nagas (nagaloka) are a plane of existence between the human and deva worlds where sense-pleasure is enjoyed.
note: nagasupannadiloke — nagas listed as a kamaloka plane alongside men and devas
samannagamo (endowed-with), anagatakala — false friends
catuvesarajjasamannagamo, ...anumanagama — false friends
Nagasuttavannana — section title; the Nagasutta (Ud) puns nago on the Buddha/elephant. Header only
ubhinnam mahananam = the two great nagas (Buddha + the elephant), in the Naga(Palileyya)sutta; honorific of the Buddha
hatthinaga/buddhanaga compared — the bull-elephant's mind matched to the buddha-naga; nagena=elephant simile vehicle
magganasamannagamena — false friend
nanagandhavanantare = nana-gandha-vana (various-scent forest); not naga
atapaniyadhammasamannagamena — false friend

claim: Nagas, like devas, cause rain to fall (naganubhavena devo vassati).
note: Uposathasutta-vannana, Anotatta lake; weather-power of nagas

claim: Among great beings of the ocean are nagas — both wave-crest dwellers and mansion-dwelling (vimana) nagas.
note: nagati umipitthivasino...vimanatthakanagapi — glosses naga as a class of sea-being
brahma-naga-yakkha-gandhabba list of beings who praise; passing mention in a list, no ontological claim
mahanaga = the great elders (mahatthera) surrounding like great nagas; honorific simile of monks
Chaddanta nagaraja with 8000 naga retinue — simile vehicle (viya); naga-king as serpent-being but a passing simile, no doctrinal claim
deva-supanna-naga-yakkha-gandhabba-manussa list of beings; passing mention
nagavikkantacaro of the Jina (lokagga) — the Buddha's noble (elephant/naga) gait; honorific of the Buddha
nagavikkantacarano of lokagga — Buddha's noble gait; honorific
nagaggaho (naga-seizing spot) listed beside yakkhaggaha/bhutaggaha — vastu-vijja site of a naga; passing mention, no doctrinal claim
bandhanagare = bandhana-agara (prison-house); not naga
moneyyadhammasamannagamena — false friend
yathavuttagunasamannagamena — false friend
kosajjasamannagamena — false friend
nagara (citizens/townsfolk) gave nothing; nagamasi = na+agamasi (false friend). Token naga is nagara=citizen + nonlexical
cakkaratanadisamannagamena — false friend
catuvesarajjasamannagamo, ...anumanagama — false friends

claim: A naga (deva-naga) asked what vow/brahmacariya yielded his iddhi, splendour and great mansion — the karma behind naga power.
note: idanca te naga mahavimanam — addressed to a naga about the merit-cause of his attainment
caram samahito nago — the concentrated 'naga' (arahant/sage) walking or standing; honorific of the composed one
samannagamena — false friend
samannagamena — false friend
saddhahattho mahanago — 'the great naga whose hand is faith'; honorific (here elephant-imagery applied to the noble person; AN 6.43)
...desanagambhiratahi = desana-gambhirata (depth of teaching); not naga
appicchatadigunasamannagamena, ...samannagatassa — false friends
...padhaniyangasamannagamena (x2) — false friends

claim: Nagas have very long bodies (many fathoms) — among the 'long' creatures of the great ocean alongside fish and monitor lizards; Rahu cited for largest body.
note: Mettasutta-a gloss of dighatta: nagamacchagodhadayo, mahasamudde naganam attabhava — naga as a sea-creature class with described body-size. (hatthinaga in same passage = elephant, but the dighatta naga is the sea-naga)
ahitadukkhanagamapatthanavasenapi = ...an-agama-patthana (non-coming); not naga
ahitadukkhanagamapatthanavasena = an-agama-patthana; not naga
nagavatimandape — the Nagavati pavilion on Manosila where yakkha-senapatis assemble; place-name with naga, passing scenery
chaddantanagakule — the Chaddanta elephant Jataka: bodhisatta born in the tusker-clan, gave his own tusk (dante chetva); naga=elephant
explicit folk etymology: nagam=punabbhavam neva gantaram nagantaram; agum na karoti ti nago; balava ti nago — the sage who does no wrong / is strong; honorific of the Buddha (Hemavatasutta)
bandhanagarani = bandhana-agara (prisons); not naga
nagamasi = na+agamasi (did not come); not naga
moneyyadhammasamannagamena — false friend
titthatu nago, ma nagam ghattesi, namo karohi nagassa (MN 1.249) — 'the naga' = the sage/Buddha to be revered; honorific
moneyyadhammasamannagamena — false friend

claim: Nagas dwelling below the Ganga (kambala, assatara nagas) came up and did honour to the Buddha.
note: hettha ganganivasino kambalassatara-adayo naga — water-dwelling naga clans worshipping; asserts their realm + capacity to honour the Buddha

claim: Naga-kings born in the Ganga made jewelled boats and led the Buddha and Sangha into the naga-realm (nagabhavana).
note: Ratanasutta-a; nagaraja, nagabhavana, nagaparisa — naga-realm, naga-assembly, capacity to honour and host the Buddha
manussa ca naga ca tathagatassa sakkaram karonti — recap that men and nagas honour; brief, but follows the claim-bearing p100. Passing mention here
nagadevabrahmanubhavena — naga/deva/brahma power named only to be denied as the cause; passing mention in a list
nagadevabrahmanubhavena — same list as p102; passing mention
naggahesum = na+aggahesum (did not accept); not naga
naggahesum = na+aggahesum; not naga
naggahesum = na+aggahesum; not naga
nagama/nagamati = na+agama (did not go); supinepi na agamasi — not naga
vipassanagabbham = vipassana-gabbha (insight-womb); not naga

claim: Shrines like Aggalava were formerly the dwellings (bhavana) of yakkhas and nagas before being converted.
note: yakkhanagadinam bhavanani — naga abodes; asserts nagas inhabit such places
susunaga in the chetva khilam verse (DN 2.332 / SN 1.37) — said of arahants ('young nagas'/those who do no wrong); honorific
nagasanghapurakkhato = honoured by a host of (war) elephants; anikagga/balakaya military context, Pabbajjasutta. Elephant-army sense
agunam akaranena nago ti pavuccati — explicit na+agu etymology; the sinless one is called naga; honorific (Sabhiyasutta)
agum na karoti kinci loke, nago pavuccate — does no wrong, called naga; honorific etymology
naga nagassa = vocative addressing the Buddha (the naga), of the naga; honorific

claim: By deva-power and naga-power rain falls at Anotatta and rivers flow.
note: Selasutta-a; devanubhavena naganubhavena ca vassanti — weather-power of nagas (parallel to s0503a.att-51_p014)

claim: The Buddha teaches even nagas and the like (nagadike) in the worldly sense, though 'gods and men' is the standard formula for the trainable.
note: nagadikepi pana esa lokiyatthena anusasati — nagas among those instructed by the Buddha
...desanagambhiratahi = desana-gambhirata; not naga
akasenagamma = akasena+agamma (coming by air); not naga
naga vandantu satthuno — 'let the nagas (=the 300 bhikkhus, just made arahants) honour the teacher'; honorific of arahants in verse
anagaro = an-agara (homeless); not naga
anagaro = an-agara (homeless); not naga
dhonadhammasamannagama — false friend (samannagama)
explicit gloss: Nago ti hatthi (Dhaniyasutta-a) — naga = elephant
nago putilatamva ...chetva — the elephant breaking the putilata creeper (bondage) simile; continuation of the hatthi gloss in p041
na tani uggayha vadeyya nago — agum na karoti etymology cited (Cnd); the naga(sage) does not grasp views; honorific (Magandiyasutta)
nagapalivethanam — a mountain name ('naga-coil') among seven peaks near Gandhamadana; place-name, passing scenery
eko care matangaranneva nago — like an elephant (matanga) in the forest; Khaggavisana, elephant simile
Nagova uppatti story: a king reborn in the elephant-womb (hatthiyoniyam) as a great tusker (mahanaga, Palileyyaka-naga); naga=elephant throughout
nagova yuthani vivajjayitva sanjatakhandho padumi — the elephant leaving the herds; Khaggavisana elephant simile
ayam hatthi...sarira-mahantataya va nago — explicitly the elephant; with a pun on nagamana/aguakarana but the base referent is the elephant
nagati bhagavantam alapanto aha — 'naga' is vocative addressing the Buddha; honorific (Mettagusutta)
saddhahattho mahanago (AN 6.43; Thag 694) — the great naga whose hand is faith; honorific
ahatamaninagadanta = mani-naga-danta (jewelled elephant-tusks); naga=elephant (tusk imagery for bristling hairs)
sutvana nagassa = having heard the naga's (Buddha's) word; nagassa tava bhagava vacanam; honorific (Bhadravudhasutta)
moneyyasamannagamena — false friend
desanagambhiro = desana-gambhira; not naga
bandhanagare = bandhana-agara (prison); not naga
sataṃ hemavata naga, isadanta urulhava, suvannakaccha matanga — a hundred Himalayan tusker-elephants (isadanta='chariot-pole tusks', matanga); naga=elephant in the gift-comparison verse
Nagavimanavannana — section title for the Naga-mansion story (Vv). Header only; the story (see p002 ff) is of a deva on a dibba elephant. Passing
Nagavimanam = the naga(elephant)-mansion; the vimana shaped as/carried by a celestial elephant; Vv story context
Nagassa dantesu duvesu nimmita — pools created on the two tusks (danta) of the naga; tusks => elephant

erravanassa viya nagarajassa imassa dvisu dantesu — like Eravana, this naga-king's two tusks; Eravana=Sakka's elephant, danta=tusks => elephant. (prov said serpent but window shows tusked elephant-king Eravana)
sataṃ hemavata naga, isadanta, suvannakaccha matanga — Himalayan tusker-elephants; gift-comparison verse (parallel to att-24_p030)
deva-naga-yakkha-manussadisu = among devas, nagas, yakkhas, men...; naga in a list of beings, passing mention
nagamimsu = na+agamimsu (did not come); not naga
Pathamanagavimanavannana — header; the First Naga-mansion (Vv) story is of a deva riding a celestial elephant (see p002, dibbanaga abhiruyha). Header
susukkakhandham abhiruyha nagam; mahantam dibbanagam abhiruyha — a deva mounting a great all-white celestial elephant; naga=elephant mount
susukkakhandham abhiruyha nagam, ...dantim... abhiruyha gajavaram — mounting the tusked (danti) elephant (gajavara); naga=elephant
Nagassa dantesu duvesu nimmita — pools on the two tusks of the naga; tusks => elephant (same Vv verse as att-46_p007)
explicit gloss: Naganti dibbam hatthinagam — naga = celestial elephant; akacinam dantim (tusks)
Dutiyanagavimanavannana — header; Second Naga-mansion, a deva on a celestial elephant (see p002 hatthinago). Header
explicit: sabbaseto mahanto dibbo hatthinago paturahosi — a great all-white celestial elephant appeared; naga=elephant
dibbanagam abhiruyha...hatthikkhandhato oruyha — mounting the celestial elephant, dismounting from the elephant's back; naga=elephant
mahantam nagam abhiruyha sabbasetam gajuttamam — mounting the great all-white supreme elephant (gajuttama); naga=elephant
Tatiyanagavimanavannana — header; Third Naga-mansion, same Vv elephant-mount genre. Header
Tatiyanagavimanam — the Third Naga(elephant)-mansion story; ko nu dibbena yanena (celestial vehicle = elephant); same Vv genre
ratha ca naga turiyani ca — chariots and elephants and music in the pleasure-park; naga=elephant (paired with ratha/turiya)
gloss: Nagati nage...rathanagaturiyani — chariots, elephants, instruments; naga=elephant
tenagama = tena+agama (then came); not naga
tassa nagassa khandhamhi — on that elephant's shoulder/back; Kunjaravimana (kunjara=elephant) context; naga=elephant
explicit gloss: Nagassati hatthinagassa — naga = elephant
nagavithiyadivithiyam — the 'naga-path' (an astronomical/celestial path of the sun-moon); naga-track name, passing mention
Nagapetavatthuvannana — header; the Naga-peta story (Pv) features a son riding a white elephant (see p014 setena nagena). Header
purato yo gacchati kunjarena, setena nagena catukkamena — going in front on a four-footed white elephant (kunjara); naga=elephant
gloss: kunjarena=hatthina; nagena (nassa agamaniyam...nago) catukkamena catuppadena — four-footed; naga=elephant
nanagotta = nana-gotta (of various clans); not naga
nanagotta(ti) = nana-vidha-gotta; explicitly glossed nanavidhagottapadesa; not naga
bandhanagare = bandhana-agara (prison); not naga
nanagandhasamerita = nana-gandha (various scents); not naga
nanagandhasamerita(ti) = nana-vidha-surabhi-gandha; not naga
nanagandhasameritam = nana-gandha; not naga
nanagandhasameritam = nana-gandha; not naga
nanagandhasameritam = nana-vidhehi gandhehi; not naga
nagara (townsfolk) following the king in giving; nagamimsu = na+agamimsu (false friend). Token = nagara=citizen + nonlexical
Nagova bandhanam chetva, viharami anasavo — like an elephant breaking its bonds; arahant's verse; elephant simile of freedom
explicit gloss: naga ti keci vadanti, nagarukkha ti attho — the naga(ironwood) tree on mountain peaks; tree-sense
nanagandhasamerita = nana-gandha; not naga
nago tuttatthito yatha — like an elephant pricked by the goad (tutta=elephant-goad); elephant simile
Nagova bandhanam chetva — like an elephant breaking its bonds; arahant verse, elephant simile
idisa te mahanaga, atula ca mahayasa...tathagata — the great nagas, the Tathagatas; honorific of Buddhas
punagamim = puna+agamim (came again); not naga
Nagova bandhanam chetva — like an elephant breaking bonds; arahant verse, elephant simile
akasenagama = akasena+agama (came by air); not naga
manussa-deva-naga-garula-kumbhanda-yakkha-gandhabba list of beings who built a stupa; naga in a being-list, passing mention
naga tattha samagantva...manussa ceva deva ca buddhathupam akamsu — nagas assembling to build the Buddha-stupa; passing list with men and devas
manussa devanaga ca buddhapujam akamsu — men, devas, nagas doing puja; passing list
manussa ceva deva ca, naga ca garula tatha — naga in a list of beings; passing mention
manussa devanaga ca, garula ca kumbhandaka — naga in a list of beings; passing mention
manuja devata naga, kumbhanda garula tatha — naga in a list of beings; passing mention
saddhadijagaradhammasamannagamena — false friend (samannagamena)
Nago sangamasiseva, sato tatradhivasaye — like a war-elephant at the battle-front; elephant simile (Yasoja, parallel Thag)
explicit gloss: ajaneyyo hatthinago yuddhamandale...vidhamseti — a thoroughbred war-elephant; naga=elephant
nagasatasahassani sabbalankarabhusita, tidhapabhinna matanga — a hundred-thousand caparisoned rutting elephants (matanga, tidhapabhinna=three-channel musth); naga=elephant
aruyha sirikam nagam, nagara nikkhamim — mounting the royal elephant, left the city; naga=royal elephant (Upali's past-life)
pesetva sirikam nagam...nago nuddharate padam — sending the royal elephant against the Buddha, the elephant would not lift its foot; naga=elephant
nagam runnamanam disva — seeing the elephant down-hearted; continuation of the elephant-against-Buddha story; naga=elephant
nanagandhe = nana-gandhe (various scents); not naga
Nagova bandhanam chetva — like an elephant breaking bonds; arahant verse, elephant simile
sihamvekacaram nagam (SN 1.30; Sn 168) — 'the naga roaming solitary like a lion'; honorific of the Buddha (Nidanagatha)
devanagayakkhanam dhammam desentam — teaching Dhamma to devas, nagas, yakkhas; naga in a being-list audience, passing mention
explicit gloss: sangamavacaro hatthinago viya — like a war-elephant at the battle-front; naga=elephant

claim: Long-lived nagas, possessed of iddhi and great fame, come under the speaker's sway by the fruit of his merit.
note: yepi dighayuka naga, iddhimanto mahayasa, tepi mam vasamanventi — asserts naga longevity + iddhi + that they submit (Suppiya). dighayuka + iddhimanto = ontological claim
tidhapabhinnam matangam kunjaram...bali nago upagantva yutha niharate gajam — a strong elephant (matanga/kunjara/gaja) driving another from the herd; naga=elephant (Subhuti)
upagatam mahanagam, devadevam narasabham — the great naga, god of gods, bull of men; honorific of the Buddha
Nagova bandhanam chetva — like an elephant breaking bonds; arahant verse, elephant simile
Sangamasise nagova, bahu pane vihethayam — like a war-elephant at the battle-front harming many beings; elephant simile (Vappa)
nagam paggayha renuhi vipassissabhiropayim — honouring the naga (Buddha Vipassi) with pollen/powder; purakkhatam savakehi; honorific of the Buddha
aggamagganasamannagamena — false friend (samannagamena)
nagavikkantagamano (with sihahanu, brahmagiro) — 'of elephant/naga-noble gait'; one of the Buddha's marks; honorific of the Buddha
